title: scaevola auriculata
intro: Scaevola auriculata, also known as the finger grass or blue fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and as an antiseptic to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Scaevola auriculata is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
